evidence: All HTTP handler classes use plural -Handlers suffix uniformly: SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, VisualizationHandlers
Utility classes use -Utils suffix consistently: CqlUtils, MathUtils, RelationUtils, HttpApiUtils — except LogDiceCalculator which uses -Calculator
Java naming conventions (camelCase methods, PascalCase types, SCREAMING_SNAKE constants) applied uniformly throughout all packages
Record types used consistently for pure value objects; non-record classes documented in model/package-info.java with explicit rationale
Package-private visibility applied consistently to implementation classes across api/, exploration/ packages
Enum label() + toString() override pattern applied consistently across RelationEdgeType, SharingCategory, PosGroup
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: LogDiceCalculator uses a -Calculator suffix while CqlUtils, MathUtils, RelationUtils, HttpApiUtils all use -Utils, creating a single naming style island in the utils package.

strengths: All HTTP handler classes use the plural -Handlers suffix consistently — a developer seeing SketchHandlers can correctly predict the naming of ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers
Enum label() + toString() pattern applied identically in RelationEdgeType, SharingCategory, and PosGroup — stable API serialization keys are consistent across all three enums
Package-private access modifier used consistently for implementation classes — api/, exploration/ both enforce public surface minimisation through the same mechanism
Record types used for pure data throughout model/ and exploration/; the two non-record classes in model/ are explicitly documented with rationale in package-info.java
camelCase/PascalCase Java naming conventions applied uniformly — no snake_case or Hungarian notation anywhere
convention_outlier.issue_character: A single isolated naming convention outlier in the utils package: LogDiceCalculator deviates from the -Utils suffix used by all other utility classes.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: Convention consistency is strong across this codebase — patterns for handler naming, enum serialization, visibility, and type representation are applied uniformly. The score reaches 90 (strong, mostly consistent with minor style islands that don't cause real confusion) because the only outlier is the -Calculator vs -Utils suffix for one utility class. This is a minor friction point that would not mislead a developer about behavior, but it does create a slight 'why is this one different?' moment in the utils package.

issues.summary: LogDiceCalculator uses -Calculator suffix while all peer utility classes use -Utils
evidence: Five utility classes end in -Utils: CqlUtils, MathUtils, RelationUtils, HttpApiUtils, RelationPatternBuilder (static-only final class)
LogDiceCalculator is a static-only final class with no state, identical in structure to MathUtils — only the suffix differs
A developer searching for 'math utilities' would look for MathUtils-style naming and might not find LogDiceCalculator
issues.suggestion: Rename LogDiceCalculator to LogDiceUtils to match the -Utils convention used by all other static utility classes in the codebase. Update the single reference in CollocateQueryHelper.java.

root_causes: LogDiceCalculator was likely named before the -Utils convention solidified; the convention was never back-applied to this class
